content.problemStatement:
Chef and Chefina are competing against each other in a programming contest. They were both able to solve all the problems in the contest, so the winner between them must be decided by time penalty. Chef solved all the problems in XXX minutes and made PPP wrong submissions, while Chefina solved all the problems in YYY minutes and made QQQ wrong submissions. Who won the competition (or was it a draw)?

**Note:** The time penalty is calculated as follows:


The base time penalty is the time at which the final problem was solved (so, XXX for Chef and YYY for Chefina)
Each wrong submission adds a penalty of 101010 minutes
The winner is the person with less penalty time. If they both have the same penalty, it is considered a draw.

Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains four space-separated integers — X,Y,PX,Y,PX,Y,P, and QQQ respectively.


Output Format
For each test case, output a single line containing one string — the name of the winner ("Chef" or "Chefina"), or "Draw" if the match was a draw. Print each string without the quotes.


Each character of the answer can be printed in either uppercase or lowercase, i.e, the strings "Chef", "chEf", "cHeF", etc. are treated as equivalent.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains four space‑separated integers X Y P Q — the time (in minutes) Chef solved the last problem, the time Chefina solved the last problem, the number of wrong submissions made by Chef, and the number of wrong submissions made by Chefina respectively.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing one of the strings "Chef", "Chefina" or "Draw" (case‑insensitive) indicating who has the smaller total penalty time, or that the contest ends in a draw.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: Chef penalty = 10 + 2*10 = 30, Chefina penalty = 8 + 3*10 = 38 → Chef wins.\nTest case 2: Both penalties = 10 + 10*10 = 110 → Draw.\nTest case 3: Chef penalty = 40 + 2*10 = 60, Chefina penalty = 21 + 0*10 = 21 → Chefina wins.

solution.approach: For each test case compute the total penalty for Chef as X + 10*P and for Chefina as Y + 10*Q. Compare the two values: if Chef's penalty is smaller, print "Chef"; if Chefina's is smaller, print "Chefina"; otherwise print "Draw". This is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – a constant amount of work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
